name: Umar ibn Ali
title: Brother's Companion
shortDescription: The son of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and loyal brother of the Master of Martyrs who fought manfully alongside other Hashemite youths on Ashura.
fullStory:
Umar ibn Ali (AS), known as Umar al-Atraf, was the son of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and Sahba al-Taghlibiyya. He was the half-brother of Imam Hussain (AS) and had a special love and devotion for him.

Although some relatives in Medina tried to dissuade him from accompanying Imam Hussain (AS), Umar decided to accompany his brother's caravan to Karbala and not leave him alone against the oppressors.

On the day of Ashura, after seeking permission from the Master of Martyrs (AS), Umar ibn Ali entered the battlefield with unparalleled bravery. He recited epic war poetry reflecting his noble lineage and deep faith, and dealt fatal blows to Umar Sa'd's soldiers with his sword.

After a long and heroic battle, he too, like his other brothers and nephews, was surrounded by the enemy and attained the great honor of martyrdom through spear and sword strikes.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عُمَرَ بْنِ أَميرِ المُؤْمِنينَ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Umar, the son of Amir al-Mu'minin.
source: Manaqib Al Abi Talib, Ibn Shahr Ashub
